message:
feat(btree,c-api,core,vdbe,wal): interior-node deletion, size-prefixed C allocator, FEC bounds checks, and time-travel codegen wiring

btree/cursor.rs:
- Implement replace_interior_cell for in-place interior node cell
  replacement with overflow chain management
- Implement full interior-node deletion: advance to successor leaf,
  delete successor, re-seek original key, replace interior cell with
  successor payload. Handles root collapse edge case.

c-api/lib.rs:
- Fix undefined behavior in libc_malloc/libc_free: prefix allocations
  with an 8-byte size header so sqlite3_free can reconstruct the Layout.
  Previous code used a 1-byte Layout for dealloc which is UB per the
  global allocator contract.

core/db_fec.rs:
- Use saturating arithmetic in serialized_size_for to prevent overflow
  with malicious group_size values
- Add bounds check before allocating repair symbol vec to defend against
  OOM from crafted sidecar r_repair values

core/connection.rs:
- Remove schemas field from SelectProgram (moved to engine)

vdbe/codegen.rs:
- Thread time_travel parameter through codegen_select_full_scan so
  SetSnapshot is emitted for full-scan temporal queries

vdbe/engine.rs:
- Add time_travel_cursors HashMap and time_travel_marker/time_travel_cursors
  accessor methods to VdbeEngine

wal/wal_fec.rs:
- Add bounds check on k_source in WalFecGroupMeta::from_bytes to prevent
  OOM from maliciously large values in corrupted WAL sidecar data

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -859,6 +859,7 @@ pub fn codegen_select(
                 cursor,
                 table,
                 table_alias,
+                time_travel,
                 schema,
                 columns,
                 where_clause.as_deref(),
@@ -975,6 +976,7 @@ pub fn codegen_select(
             cursor,
             table,
             table_alias,
+            time_travel,
             schema,
             columns,
             where_clause.as_deref(),
@@ -1007,6 +1009,7 @@ fn codegen_select_full_scan(
     cursor: i32,
     table: &TableSchema,
     table_alias: Option<&str>,
+    time_travel: Option<&TimeTravelClause>,
     schema: &[TableSchema],
     columns: &[ResultColumn],
     where_clause: Option<&Expr>,
